controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
feature_name: PCIe 6.0 64.0 GT/s Link with Flit Mode
description: Supports 64.0 GT/s operation with PCIe 6.0 Flit Mode for high-throughput communication. Provides Physical Layer 64G Extended Capability with control and status registers. Enables flit-based datapath and associated link management. Improves efficiency and latency at very high speeds. Backward-compatible behavior depends on configuration.
evidence: 64.0 GT/s Physical Layer Extended Capability: PL64G_CAPABILITY/CONTROL/STATUS (B+0x4/0x8/0xc); Device-Wide Flit Mode Controller Options (page 141)
dependencies: Requires 64G-capable PHY and correct flit-mode enablement; dependent on CX_GEN speed and PHY configuration
Driver required: Standard OS PCIe stack; no special driver beyond PCIe 6.0 support

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Interfaces to an external SerDes PHY via PIPE. Allows selection of a custom PHY through configuration parameters. Includes advanced PHY timing and equalization settings. Provides a PHY register bus and signal mapping for integration. Ensures electrical layer compliance via external PHY capabilities.
evidence: Parameter Descriptions: Phy Type/Selection; Advanced PHY Config (pages ~208–214); PHY Register Bus Interface Signals (page 1198)
dependencies: Requires external PIPE-compliant PHY and proper clocking/reset sequencing
Remarks: PHY specifics depend on chosen vendor implementation

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements the Secondary PCIe Capability for additional link controls. Provides Link Control 3 and Lane Error Status registers. Exposes per-lane error and maintenance bits beyond base capability. Enhances Gen3+ equalization and link management. Useful for diagnostics and fine-tuning.
dependencies: Exists when (CX_GEN3_SPEED && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2)

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides vendor and spec-defined controls for link training and equalization. Includes Gen3-related knobs and EQ coefficient programming. Offers vendor-specific DLLP generation and port force mechanisms for testing. Facilitates achieving stable links across speeds and channels. Supports reviewing EQ status through dedicated registers.
evidence: DWC_PCIE_DSP: GEN3_RELATED_OFF (+0x890), GEN3_EQ_LOCAL_FS_LF_OFF (+0x894), GEN3_EQ_PSET_COEF_MAP__i; VENDOR_SPEC_DLLP_OFF (+0x704); PORT_FORCE_OFF (+0x708)
dependencies: Requires link to be in training or recovery states for effect; vendor sequences recommended
Driver required: Platform bring-up tooling or debug driver

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Exposes standard ASPM controls for L0s and L1 power states. Configurable via Link Control registers. Works with endpoint and platform policies to reduce idle power. Can be tuned alongside L1 Substates for deeper savings. Impacts exit latency and performance.
evidence: PCIe Capability: LINK_CONTROL_LINK_STATUS (B+0x10); DWC_PCIE_DSP: ACK_F_ASPM_CTRL_OFF (+0x70c), PORT_LINK_CTRL_OFF (+0x710)
dependencies: Requires ASPM enabled in both Root Port and Endpoint; may require OS policy
Remarks: Balance power vs latency per system policy

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements PCIe L1 Substates for deeper idle power reduction. Includes control registers for entry thresholds and common-mode restore (CPM). Coordinates with acceptable latency reporting to maintain QoS. Provides fine-grained management for link idle behavior. Enhances battery life and thermal headroom in low-activity scenarios.
evidence: PF0_L1SUB_CAP: L1SUB_CAPABILITY, CONTROL1, CONTROL2 (B+0x4/0x8/0xc); Device-Wide L1 Substates/L1.CPM (page 122)
dependencies: Exists when CX_L1_SUBSTATES_ENABLE && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2; depends on L1 acceptable latency fields
Remarks: Endpoint support required for maximum benefit

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports internal clock gating to reduce dynamic power. Programmable control toggles unused blocks off during idle. Complements ASPM/L1SS by saving on internal logic. Minimizes power without sacrificing wake responsiveness. Useful across active and deep idle modes.
dependencies: Requires appropriate clock domains and safe gating configuration
Remarks: Use vendor guidance to avoid gating critical clocks

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements standardized power budgeting reporting. Allows software to read device power classifications and requirements. Provides select/data registers for power metrics. Helps system-level power allocation and policy enforcement. Useful in servers and multi-function devices.
dependencies: Exists when PWR_BUDGET_CAP_ENABLE && CX_PB_NFUNC>0 && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE==2 || 0)

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides mechanisms to quickly reduce device power on demand. Advertises support and whether initialization is required. Can be used during platform power emergencies to throttle. Coordinates with link and internal resources to meet limits. Complements other power management features.
evidence: Device-Wide PCIe Capability Params: PCIE_CAP2_EMERGENCY_PWR_RED_SUPPORT, PCIE_CAP2_EMERGENCY_PWR_RED_INIT_REQUIRED
dependencies: Feature bits must be enabled; platform policy controls triggers
Remarks: Behavior may be vendor- or policy-specific

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports NPEM to monitor and control enclosure functions. Exposes capability, control, and status registers. Enables management of power, cooling, and status indicators over PCIe. Integrates with platform management software. Particularly relevant for chassis-based systems.
evidence: PF0_NPEM_CAP: EXT_CAP_HDR, CAP, CTRL, STATUS (B+0x0/0x4/0x8/0xc; pages ~137)
dependencies: Exists when NPEM_ENABLE; depends on memory map type

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides a vendor-specific DMA capability structure. Advertises unrolled address offsets for DMA engine blocks. Enables high-throughput data movement without CPU intervention. Can support multiple channels depending on configuration. Useful for moving data between host memory and device buffers efficiently.
dependencies: Exists when CC_DMA_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE==2 || 0)
Driver required: Vendor DMA driver or framework integration

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports TPH for steering completions to optimal CPU resources. Advertises requester capabilities and modes. Can reduce cache misses and improve NUMA locality. Useful in multi-core systems with heavy PCIe traffic. Integrates with OS/driver TPH policies.
dependencies: Exists when CX_TPH_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE==2 || 0)

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements LTR to communicate tolerable service latencies. Helps platform coordinate power states without hurting QoS. Exposes LTR latency registers for min/max bounds. Works with L1SS and ASPM decisions. Enables deeper power savings when workloads permit.
dependencies: Requires platform firmware/OS LTR support; endpoint must honor
Remarks: Tuning required per device use case

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports PTM for time synchronization with peers. Enables timestamping and coordinated operations over PCIe. Useful for latency-sensitive and time-aware applications. Integrates with PTM capability negotiation. Improves determinism in distributed systems.
evidence: Device-Wide Options: Precision Time Management Support (page 130)
dependencies: Requires PTM-enabled peers and OS support
Remarks: Optional; not all ecosystems use PTM

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Advertises completion support for PCIe AtomicOps. Enables fine-grained synchronization across devices. Useful for shared memory programming models. Improves performance for lockless data structures. Must be used with care to avoid contention.
evidence: PCIe Capability bits: PCIE_CAP_32_ATOMIC_CPL_SUPP/64/128 (Device-Wide Params list)
dependencies: Requires peer support; may require AER if combined with ECRC/ARI

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports 10-bit Requester/Completer tags to increase outstanding transactions. Enhances parallelism and bandwidth utilization. Useful for high-latency links and deep pipelines. Requires end-to-end support. Exposed via capability bits in Device Capabilities 2.
dependencies: Requires both ends to support 10-bit tags

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports OBFF signaling to coordinate device-host buffering. Helps defer work for better power/performance. Provides capability advertisement and control. Integrates with platform policies. Useful for bursty workloads.
dependencies: Requires OS/firmware policy and endpoint readiness

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports extended TLP formats beyond base. Enables features like E2E TLP Prefix and larger headers. Required for PASID and tunneling capabilities. Ensures compatibility with advanced PCIe features. Advertised via capability bits.
dependencies: May depend on PASID/TLP Prefix enablement
Remarks: Coordinate with peers for correct operation

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports E2E TLP Prefix to carry PASID and context information. Enables process-address-space tagging for I/O virtualization. Facilitates fine-grained isolation and steering. Improves security by binding transactions to address spaces. Advertised through PASID capability defaults and TLP Prefix support.
evidence: TLP Prefix Support (page 127); PASID Capability Register Defaults (page 129)
dependencies: Requires OS/IOMMU support and endpoint capability
Remarks: Security and scalability benefits in multi-tenant systems

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides requester-side ATS for address translation caching. Reduces IOMMU translation overhead by caching results. Improves performance for repetitive access patterns. Requires coordination with system IOMMU. Exposed as an extended capability per function.
evidence: Programming References: ATS (page 149); SII: ATS Capability Signals (page 1201)
dependencies: Requires IOMMU and OS ATS support
Remarks: Use with PASID for per-process translations

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements ARI to extend function numbering beyond 8 functions. Enables dense SR-IOV and multi-function devices. Managed via ARI capability and control registers. Interacts with AER/ECRC requirements. Improves scalability of function enumeration.
evidence: ARI Capability: ARI_BASE (B+0x0), CAP_REG (B+0x4); Device-Wide note: AER required if ARI enabled
dependencies: Requires ARI enable in both ends; may require AER

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides ACS for peer-to-peer transaction control and isolation. Offers control over request redirect, completion blocking, and more. Essential for secure virtualization. Exposes standard ACS capability structure. Enforced by platform PCIe fabric.
dependencies: Requires platform ACS enforcement; OS support

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports Single Root I/O Virtualization with multiple VFs per PF. Provides VF BARs and capability pointers. Enables scalable resource partitioning across guests or processes. Integrates with ARI for larger function counts. Requires comprehensive driver support for PF and VFs.
evidence: Device-Wide SR-IOV Related Features (page 112); PF0 SR-IOV Register Configuration (page 185); VF BARs (page 188)
dependencies: Platform IOMMU and virtualization stack; ARI recommended
Remarks: Configuration heavy; depends on resource sizing

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements MSI capability per function. Supports programmable vectors and address/data formats. Reduces latency and improves scalability over INTx. Configured through MSI capability registers. Widely supported across OSes.
dependencies: Requires MSI enable in OS and device; interrupt remapping optional

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements MSI-X with table and PBA structures. Provides larger vector space and per-vector masking. Enables fine-grained interrupt steering. Improves performance on multi-core systems. Requires correct BAR mapping for table access.
evidence: PF MSI-X Register Configuration (page 144); MSI-X PBA Offset and BIR (B+0x8)
dependencies: Requires MSI-X enable; BAR for MSI-X table must be accessible
Remarks: Ensure PBA/table memory is not cached incorrectly

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides legacy INTx signaling for compatibility. Uses Interrupt Line/Pin fields in configuration space. Higher latency and shared semantics compared to MSI/MSI-X. Still useful for early bring-up and minimal systems. Should be disabled when MSI/MSI-X are in use.
evidence: Type1 Header: BRIDGE_CTRL_INT_PIN_INT_LINE (B+0x3c); VF Interrupt Pin/Line (B+0x3c)
dependencies: None, but platform must route legacy INT lines

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Integrates MSI reception status and control blocks. Exposes multiple interrupt status vectors for handling. Aids in debug of MSI delivery and routing. Provides optional GPIO/IO interfacing for interrupts. Useful for validation and platform-specific integrations.
dependencies: Depends on MSI being used; platform interrupt fabric required

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements PCIe AER for detailed error logging and reporting. Provides correctable, non-fatal, and fatal error status/control. Enables ECRC error reporting when enabled. Supports error injection and diagnostics per spec. Essential for robust RAS in production systems.
dependencies: Exists when AER_ENABLE && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2; required if ECRC or ARI enabled

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports reporting of surprise link down events. Helps firmware/OS respond to sudden device removal or power loss. Part of optional protocol checks in device-wide options. Improves system resilience under fault conditions. Works with AER and link status.
evidence: Device-Wide: Surprise Down Error Reporting option (PCIe spec 7.8.6 reference)
dependencies: Depends on capability enablement and link monitoring

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Adds datapath protection features via vendor-specific capability. Can include parity/ECC style protections on internal paths. Exposes status/control for protected blocks. Enhances reliability in harsh or high-uptime environments. Complements AER for end-to-end coverage.
dependencies: Exists when CX_RAS_EN && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE==2 || 0)

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides per-lane error status for link diagnostics. Helps pinpoint physical layer issues. Useful for board bring-up and signal integrity tuning. Exposed under Secondary PCIe capability. Can trigger remediation sequences.
dependencies: Requires Secondary PCIe capability present
Remarks: Use with EQ controls for faster convergence

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
feature_name: Vendor Analytics: Event Counters and Time-Based Analysis
description: Includes vendor-specific event counters to profile activity. Provides time-based analysis controls and data registers. Helps quantify bandwidth, latency, and utilization. Useful for performance tuning and regression testing. Complements standard PCIe counters.
dependencies: None beyond register access
Driver required: Vendor perf tools or custom scripts

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Allows crafting vendor-specific DLLPs for validation. Provides port force options to drive link states. Useful for compliance testing and corner-case exploration. Should be restricted in production. Assists in reproducing protocol scenarios.
dependencies: Requires privileged access; may disrupt normal traffic
Remarks: Use only on isolated test systems

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Exposes internal equalization status across multiple registers. Aids in correlating link training outcomes with settings. Useful for hardware validation and SI analysis. Complements lane error status and EQ controls. Provides deeper visibility for root-causing issues.
dependencies: None beyond access to USP debug space
Remarks: Register locations may vary by build

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides DBI2 shadow copies of PCIe capability registers. Enables safe updates while preserving original values. Useful for firmware patching and late changes. Assists in auditing default capability settings. Enhances robustness of configuration routines.
dependencies: Always present per PF_PCIE_CAP_DBI2i

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
feature_name: Per-Function PCIe Capability and Type1 Header
description: Implements standard PCIe Capability per function with Device/Link/SLOT regs. Provides Type1 header for bridge/switch modes with standard fields. Ensures compliance and interoperability with PCIe enumeration. Exposes status and control for device and link. Forms the base for all higher capabilities.
evidence: PF_PCIE_CAPi: DEVICE/LINK CAPS & CONTROL; DWC_PCIE_DSP: TYPE1_* header regs
dependencies: Always present; device type dependent (EP/RP/DM/SW)

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports programmable BARs for PFs and VFs. Allows sizing and mapping of MMIO spaces. Provides defaults and programming sequences. Critical for exposing device resources to host. Integrates with SR-IOV for VF BAR mapping.
evidence: Per-Function PCIe Capabilities and BAR Setup (PF0 pages 160–165); VF BARs (page 188)
dependencies: Depends on function type and memory map

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements Device Serial Number extended capability. Exposes 64-bit unique identifier. Useful for asset tracking and authentication. Accessible via standard capability linked list. Can be read-only and factory-programmed.
dependencies: None

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides VPD storage access via PCIe capability. Enables reading device-specific data and calibration info. Useful for manufacturing and field service. Exposed through control/capability and data registers. Integrates with OS VPD frameworks where available.
dependencies: Exists when VPD_CAP_ENABLE && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements Device 3 Extended Capability for additional controls. Provides Device Capabilities 3 and related status. Extends configuration space for newer PCIe features. Helps future-proof capability set. Exposed via standard extended capability header.
dependencies: Exists when DEV3_CAP_ENABLE && CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE<2

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Adds Compute Express Link (CXL) transport support for RCiEP mode. Exposes PCIe DVSEC structures for CXL discovery. Enables CXL-specific control and status. Integrates with CXL software stacks. Useful for coherent memory/device extensions.
evidence: PF0_CXL_DEVICE_CAP: RCiEP PCIe EXT CAP HDR (B+0x0) and DVSEC regs
dependencies: Exists when CX_CXL_ENABLE && (CX_MEMORY_MAP_PCIE_TYPE==0 || 2)

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Provides CXL Root Complex Register Block and FlexBus Port DVSECs. Exposes capability and control/status for CXL ports. Facilitates CXL link bring-up and management. Enables CXL discovery by software. Complements RCiEP device DVSECs.
evidence: CXL_RCRB_*: PCIe EXT CAP HDR, DVSEC PORT HDR, FLEXBUS CTRL/STATUS
dependencies: Requires CX_CXL_ENABLE; platform firmware support

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
feature_name: CXL Test DVSEC with Test Lock
description: Implements CXL test features gated by a test lock. Allows access to test capabilities when unlocked. Prevents accidental or malicious use in production. Useful for validation and factory test. Integrates with DVSEC discovery.
dependencies: Requires CX_CXL_ENABLE and test unlock
Remarks: Do not enable in field units

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports PCIe Readiness signaling to indicate service availability. Implements Lightweight Notification for low-overhead events. Helps coordinate power/performance transitions with minimal traffic. Advertised via device-wide options. Useful for energy-aware scheduling.
evidence: Device-Wide Options: Readiness Support (page 105); Lightweight Notification (page 108)
dependencies: Requires OS policy support and peer understanding

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports Deferrable Memory Write transactions. Allows postponing writes to optimize power/performance. Useful in low-power platforms. Advertised via device-wide options. Requires careful coordination with software timing.
evidence: Device-Wide Option: Deferrable Memory Write Support (page 139)
dependencies: Platform policy and OS support

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Supports End-to-End CRC generation and checking on TLPs. Enhances data integrity across the fabric. Interacts with AER for error reporting. Typically configurable via Device Control bits. Improves robustness for high-speed links.
evidence: Note: AER required if ECRC or ARI enabled (Device-Wide Options)
dependencies: Requires peer support and AER enabled

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
feature_name: TLP Bypass Internal Error Reporting Signals
description: Provides internal signals for errors detected in TLP bypass paths. Assists in validation of fast-path mechanisms. Useful for deep debug during performance tuning. May expose error flags via vendor interfaces. Complements AER for internal coverage.
evidence: Signal Descriptions: TLP Bypass Internal Error Reporting Signals (page 1211)
dependencies: Requires access to internal signal/status surfaces

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Optional support for CCIX transport via DVSEC. Enables cache-coherent interconnect over PCIe when licensed. Provides discovery and configuration via DVSEC blocks. Useful in heterogeneous compute systems. Presence is configuration and license dependent.
dependencies: Requires CCIX license/enable and platform support

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Maintains capability pointer chains for PFs and VFs. Ensures discoverability of standard and extended capabilities. Required for compliant enumeration. Provides base for parsing features like MSI/AER/etc. Facilitates robust driver initialization.
dependencies: Always present per function type

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements PCIe PM capability for D0–D3hot/cold management. Provides PME generation and status. Works with ASPM/L1SS to reduce power. Exposes programmable PMCSR and related bits. Enables OS-directed power management.
dependencies: Requires OS PM support and device cooperation

controller_name: Synopsys DesignWare PCIe Embedded Host Controller (DM) v6.00a
description: Implements Type1 header fields for bridge/switch operation. Provides I/O and Prefetchable Memory window registers. Exposes Secondary/Primary bus numbers and bridge control. Enables Root Port/Downstream Port modes in DM/SW builds. Ensures correct forwarding and resource decoding.
evidence: DWC_PCIE_DSP: TYPE1_* regs (DEV/VEND ID, STATUS/COMMAND, PREF_LIMIT_UPPER, IO_LIMIT_UPPER, CAP_PTR, EXP_ROM_BASE, BRIDGE_CTRL)
dependencies: Device Type selection (EP/RP/DM/SW) and pin-select for Switch Port
